globalRules: Persist important results in project knowledge, not only in chat (save_finding / save_entity / save_open_question).
After each substantive step, call agent_record_step and propose the next action.
Use the C64RE Headless runtime for product work. VICE is internal-dev-only and never a fallback for user projects.
Do not enable C64RE_FULL_TOOLS as a normal solution.
Do not call the V3 WebSocket directly when an MCP tool exists.
Never assume repo-relative samples/ or process cwd for user media; take absolute or project-relative paths from the user.
Prefer bounded evidence queries over dumping raw traces into chat.
Do not use raw SQL (trace_store_query) as a workaround for a broken convenience reader; fix or report the reader.
Report a path-resolution failure clearly with the requested path; do not silently fall back to repo fixtures.

playbooks.title: New Project Onboarding
userIntentExamples: Make this folder a C64 project.
preconditions: An MCP session is connected.
The user has (or will provide) a working directory for the project.

steps.action: Onboard: detect new vs resumed project, load persistent memory. For a brand-new directory, initialize it first (knowledge writes are rejected until then).
steps.askHumanWhen: it is unclear whether to create a new project here

steps.action: Ask the user's objective (crack / EasyFlash port / analysis / bugfix / routine) and set role + workflow.
Drop .d64/.g64/.crt/.prg + context into the project folder (or give absolute paths).

For a resumed project, search existing knowledge before re-deriving anything: find where a topic/address/track is already described and pull together the records around a payload. Rebuild the index first if the project changed on disk.
steps.askHumanWhen: the search returns nothing for a topic you expected to exist

steps.action: Ask the orchestrator for the single next product step; run the inventory/media-sync step or follow its named tool.
Confirm next action and record the step.

stopConditions: Project is initialized, role + workflow chosen, media location known.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not skip onboarding and start disassembling blind.
Do not assume repo samples/.

playbooks.title: Media Inventory
preconditions: A project exists.
Media path (absolute or project-relative) is known.

steps.action: Inspect the medium directory/layout from the user-supplied path.
Extract files/payloads into project artifacts.
Sync the project store with disk so newly extracted files/manifests are registered, imported, and reflected in every view.
Record initial findings/questions and refresh the dashboard.

stopConditions: Medium inventoried, payloads listed, artifacts registered + views in sync.
nextActions: Choose Trace-First Runtime Discovery (crack/port), Disassembly-First Static Pass (simple payload), Raw Disk / GCR Inspection (.g64 / protection), or Cartridge Chunk Inspection (.crt banks).
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not fall back to repo samples if the path fails — report it.
Do not treat a CRT as a PRG.

playbooks.title: Raw Disk / GCR Inspection
userIntentExamples: This .g64 has copy protection — inspect the raw tracks.
The directory is sparse but the disk is full; what's in the raw sectors?
preconditions: A project exists.
A .g64 / raw disk path is known.
The DOS directory alone is not enough (protection, custom loader, orphan sectors).

steps.action: List the G64 half-track slots and inspect a track's raw GCR/sync/header structure.
Read a candidate sector and detect anomalies (weak bits, long/killer tracks).
Extract raw sectors / a raw track, or a custom-LUT payload when a loader uses a non-DOS layout.

Register a carved code-derived load (custom-loader/DD00 block with no CBM dir or on-disk LUT) as a first-class payload — pass the carved .prg, load address, format and its track/sector spans so it renders on the disk view and memory map like a normal payload.
Tag the payload's disk hint, record a finding, and sync so the disk heatmap/views update.

stopConditions: Raw track/sector inspected, at least one raw artifact extracted, a hint/finding persisted, views synced.
nextActions: Disassemble the extracted loader/payload or continue Trace-First Runtime Discovery.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not assume a standard DOS layout for a protected disk.
Do not invent sector contents — extract and cite them.

playbooks.title: Cartridge Chunk Inspection
userIntentExamples: Promote the useful cartridge chunks and link them to code.
preconditions: A project exists.
A .crt path is known with bank/chunk structure.

steps.action: Extract the cartridge, then promote useful bank/slot chunks into payload entities.
Link a chunk to its best ASM/TASS artifact (or record that none exists yet) and record packer/format metadata when known.
Sync so the cartridge layout view reflects the chunk state, and record the step.

stopConditions: Useful chunks promoted, at least one linked/annotated, cartridge views synced.
nextActions: Disassemble a promoted chunk or continue Trace-First Runtime Discovery on the cartridge.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not treat a cartridge chunk as a standalone PRG without checking its bank/load context.

playbooks.title: Trace-First Runtime Discovery
userIntentExamples: Run the game first and trace what executes.
preconditions: A project exists.
A bootable .d64/.g64/.crt path is known.
Spec 726 live trace sink is available.

steps.action: Start Headless with durable capture (trace_out to a project-relative or absolute .duckdb).
Run to a stable BASIC READY screen.
steps.snippet: runtime_session_run({ session_id, max_instructions: 2000000, until: { kind: "stable_screen", frames_stable: 3 } })

steps.action: Mark the boot phase.
Type the load+run command.
Run to a stable title/loaded screen.
steps.snippet: runtime_session_run({ session_id, max_instructions: 10000000, until: { kind: "stable_screen", frames_stable: 5 } })

steps.action: Mark loaded, finalize the trace.
Query the durable trace with convenience readers (not raw SQL).
Save findings + choose disasm candidates.
Close the session when done — stops the run loop (else it pegs a core ~100%) and frees the session.

stopConditions: A trace.duckdb exists with cpu + bus + marks; executed-PC + access sets queried; the session is closed.
nextActions: Disassembly + Trace Validation on the executed payloads.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not use raw trace_store_query because a wrapper looks broken — fix/report it.
If LOAD"*",8,1 is wrong, use LOAD"$",8 to read the directory then issue the chosen LOAD.

playbooks.title: Multi-Disk Side-Swap ("Insert side N")
userIntentExamples: Wasteland asks me to insert side 3 — drive it past the prompt.
This multi-disk game wants a disk change mid-run; swap it and continue.
preconditions: A running Headless session (runtime_session_start) booted into the game.
The other disk sides are available as project/abs paths.
You must READ THE SCREEN — the game tells you when and which side to insert.

steps.action: Read the live screen and watch for a side-change prompt (e.g. "INSERT SIDE N (RETURN)"). The screen is the source of truth for WHEN a swap is needed and WHICH side.
steps.snippet: runtime_render_screen({ session_id, out_path: "/tmp/<sess>-prompt.png" })  // then read the PNG

steps.action: Open the drive door: EJECT the current disk. The running 1541 senses the disk was pulled out (write-protect line) — do NOT use the atomic runtime_media_swap here; a polling game needs the drive to see the removal over real cycles.
Let the drive run so it registers the disk-out (advance the session).
Insert the requested side: MOUNT the new disk image. The running 1541 senses a disk is now present.
Run so the drive registers the new disk, then send the RETURN the prompt asked for, then run on.

Read the screen again to confirm the prompt is gone and the game advanced (new map/scene). If it still shows the prompt, the drive did not register the change — run more cycles between eject and insert, or report a first-divergence trace.

stopConditions: The screen advanced past the "Insert side N" prompt onto game/map content after the eject → run → mount → run → RETURN sequence.
nextActions: Continue the run / trace the post-swap load, or repeat for the next requested side.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not use the atomic runtime_media_swap to answer a polling "Insert side N" prompt — it detaches+attaches with zero drive cycles between, so the running 1541 never senses the removal+insertion. Eject → run → mount → run instead.
Do not guess WHICH side — read it off the screen.
Do not assume the swap worked — render the screen and verify the prompt cleared.

playbooks.title: Disassembly-First Static Pass
userIntentExamples: Give me the code for ./loader.prg first.
preconditions: A PRG payload path is known.
The payload is simple enough to read before running, or the user asked for code first.

steps.action: Heuristic analysis pass.
Disassemble + resolve ROM/symbol references.
Draft annotations; promote to findings.

stopConditions: A readable disassembly + draft annotations exist.
nextActions: Validate with a targeted trace (Disassembly + Trace Validation).
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not claim labels/branches are correct without later runtime evidence for non-trivial code.

playbooks.title: Disassembly + Trace Validation
userIntentExamples: Which of these routines actually run?
preconditions: A disassembly exists.
The payload can be run in Headless.

steps.action: Start a targeted trace (narrow trace_domains) and mark candidate phases.
Read executed PCs + memory access sets; resolve PCs to source.
Update annotations/findings with evidence references.

stopConditions: Annotations cite executed-PC / access evidence; dead labels removed.
nextActions: Frozen Visual Inspect or Change/Patch iteration.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not use VICE; the Headless trace is the product evidence.

playbooks.title: Human-Assisted Loader / Protection Investigation
userIntentExamples: Press fire when I tell you and trace the loader.
It asks for a password / disk flip.
preconditions: A session with trace_out is running.
The human can provide input on request.

steps.action: Start Headless with trace_out.
Tell the LLM what to press / when to continue / which disk.
steps.askHumanWhen: interaction is required (fire, menu, password, disk swap)

steps.action: Drive input and stamp marks for human-observed phases.
Query IEC / $DD00 / drive PC / bus around the marks.
Record findings + open questions where human context is missing.

stopConditions: Loader/protection phases marked + evidence captured; open questions recorded.
nextActions: Change/Patch iteration or deeper disassembly of the loader.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not guess protection behaviour — capture it and ask the human for missing context.

playbooks.title: Frozen Visual Inspect To Code/Data
userIntentExamples: This logo — where do the bytes come from?
preconditions: A session can be paused at the visible state.
A trace may exist for write provenance.

steps.action: Capture a checkpoint + render the screen.
Resolve a pixel/cell to VIC/RAM evidence; read the backing RAM.
Query trace writes around the frame/mark to find the producing code.
Link the visual evidence to RAM/file/payload/disassembly.

stopConditions: Visible asset linked to producing bytes/code with evidence.
forbiddenShortcuts: Do not infer asset origin from prose; cite VIC/RAM/trace evidence.

playbooks.title: Change / Patch / Crack / Port Iteration
preconditions: The relevant code/data is understood with evidence.
A baseline trace/checkpoint exists for before/after comparison.

steps.action: Record the intended change, assumptions and risk.
Apply the change (load patched PRG / re-mount) and run with trace_out.
Compare before/after evidence.
Record success/failure + next branch; verify a rebuild where applicable.

stopConditions: Change applied + result proven by runtime/trace; result recorded.
nextActions: Iterate on the next branch or validate the full title.
forbiddenShortcuts: Dedicated code-overlay/branch tooling (Spec 711/712) is not yet exposed; do not claim it exists.
Do not skip before/after evidence.

playbooks.title: Internal Dev Oracle / VICE
userIntentExamples: (C64RE developer) Compare our drive timing against VICE.
preconditions: You are developing/debugging the C64RE MCP/core itself.
A specific internal dev fixture/artifact is named (not an implicit repo sample).

steps.action: Capture the Headless behaviour first (this is the product authority).
Run the VICE oracle on the SAME named fixture and compare boundary lanes.
steps.askHumanWhen: the divergence fixture is not explicitly named

steps.action: Record the first divergence as a finding with an oracle reference.

stopConditions: First divergence identified with an oracle reference.
nextActions: Fix the port per the C→TS forensic doctrine; re-validate with Headless.
forbiddenShortcuts: Never expose VICE as a product workflow path.
Never use a VICE trace as a replacement for Headless evidence.
Never tell an external LLM/user to switch to VICE.
Never use an implicit repo-sample fixture.

playbooks.title: Operator / Maintenance
preconditions: The project store itself needs repair/backfill/dedupe/import.
This is NOT a normal analysis question.

steps.action: Confirm the maintenance operation before any destructive/bulk change.
steps.askHumanWhen: always, before destructive or bulk changes

steps.action: Run the specific advanced maintenance tool (requires C64RE_FULL_TOOLS).
Record what was changed.

forbiddenShortcuts: Do not use maintenance tools to answer normal analysis questions.
Do not run destructive/bulk ops without explicit confirmation.
